mappings.rationaleAI: Garibaldi and the Risorgimento cover Italian unification including the Expedition of the Thousand, Cavour's diplomacy, and the creation of the Kingdom of Italy, directly matching B.2.a.i-ii on Italian unification and B.3.f.i on Cavour and unification under Victor Emmanuel.

Bismarck's wars against Denmark, Austria, and France, the proclamation of the German Empire, the Kulturkampf, and anti-socialist laws directly match B.2.a.iii on Bismarck's creation of the German Empire and B.3.c on Prussian and German developments from 1850-1920.

The Siege of Paris (1870-71) and the Paris Commune cover France's defeat by Germany, the Commune's revolutionary social programme, and its suppression during Bloody Week, directly matching B.3.b.ii on the siege of Paris, the Commune, and the establishment of the Third Republic.
The revolutions of 1848 swept across France, the German states, the Austrian Empire, and the Italian peninsula, directly matching A.2.h.ii on general European unrest and revolutions of 1848.

The Congress of Vienna (1814-1815) covers Metternich, Castlereagh, Talleyrand, the principles of legitimacy and balance of power, and the Concert of Europe, directly matching A.1.c.v and A.2.h.i on the Congress system.
Napoleon's Hundred Days covers his return from Elba, the Waterloo campaign, and Napoleon's final defeat, directly matching A.1.c.v on the Hundred Days and Napoleon's final defeat at Waterloo.

The social consequences of the Industrial Revolution including urbanization, child labour, trade unions, Chartism, and Marxism directly match A.2.a on the Industrial Revolution and A.2.a.iii on social consequences.
The Corn Laws and their repeal in 1846 under Peel directly match A.2.i.ii on political and social reform measures including repeal of the Corn Laws.
The Irish Rebellion of 1798 and the resulting Act of Union 1801 directly match A.1.e.ii on suppression of the Irish Rebellion and union of Great Britain and Ireland.
The Battle of Trafalgar (1805) was the decisive naval victory establishing British naval supremacy, directly matching A.1.c.iii on the battles of Trafalgar and Austerlitz.

Rosa Luxemburg's role in the German Social Democratic Party, Spartacist League, opposition to WWI, and murder during the 1919 German revolution directly match B.3.c.iv-v on Social Democrats and the establishment of the German Republic.

The Russo-Japanese War (1904-05) and its contribution to the 1905 Revolution in Russia directly match B.2.b.ii on Franco-Russian alliance and Russia's defeat in the Far East, and B.3.e.iv on disorders following the Russo-Japanese War.
The Berlin Conference of 1884-85 regulated European colonization in Africa and created the Congo Free State, directly matching B.2.c.ii on the scramble for Africa and European penetration.

mappings.episodeTitle: The Economic Consequences of the Peace
mappings.rationaleAI: Keynes's Economic Consequences of the Peace critiques the Treaty of Versailles reparations, directly matching B.2.d.vi on the Paris Peace Conference, German reparations, and the peace treaty.
Annie Besant's Fabian socialism, the matchgirls' strike, and trade unionism connect to B.3.a.iii on Gladstone's liberalism, Fabian socialism, and growth of the Labour movement.
Chartism's People's Charter and campaign for democratic reform directly match A.2.i.ii on the Chartist movement.
The Boxer Rebellion (1899-1901) and the international military response relate to B.2.c.ii on European penetration of Asia and economic penetration of China.
David Ricardo's theory of comparative advantage and role in the Corn Laws debate shaped 19th-century British trade policy, relevant to the Industrial Revolution context and British political reform.
